mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator designed to simplify the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It provides custom resource definitions for managing Ray clusters, jobs, and services, making it ideal for machine learning workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay using Helm, deploying the necessary components to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay Installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeRay components to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kuberay' namespace to verify they are running.

steps.title: Create a RayCluster
steps.description:
This step creates a RayCluster custom resource to start a Ray cluster managed by KubeRay.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

```

> Creates a RayCluster with 1 head and 2 worker nodes.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: ray.io/v1alpha1
kind: RayCluster
metadata:
  name: my-ray-cluster
  namespace: kuberay
spec:
  head:
    replicas: 1
  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify RayCluster Status
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RayCluster to ensure it is running as expected.

```bash

kubectl get raycluster my-ray-cluster -n kuberay

```

> Displays the status of the created RayCluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, including RayClusters, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the 'kuberay' namespace.
> Deletes the 'kuberay' namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation afterward.

```bash

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Upgrades KubeRay to version 1.5.1.
> Verifies that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/ray.io_rayclusters.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** One or more pods are failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay' and adjust resource requests/limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay Helm chart may not be installed or the namespace may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure KubeRay is installed in the correct namespace using 'helm list --namespace kuberay'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified RayCluster resource may not exist or may have been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify the RayCluster exists using 'kubectl get raycluster -n kuberay'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new Ray features and improved stability of RayCluster management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary:
You should see the RayCluster status as 'Running' when everything is working, with output similar to: 'NAME          STATUS   AGE
my-ray-cluster   Running   5m'.

spec:

  worker:
    replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources and the necessary tools installed.
